title: Replace The Tipper Assembly
summary: Remove an existing tipper and install a replacement tipper on the existing sub-plate using the documented service disconnection and reconnection steps. The source-supported flow includes lockout/tagout, PPE, optional side-panel removal for access, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing the mounting screws, installing the replacement tipper, reconnecting services, reinstalling removed components as needed, and restarting the operator station.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when the tipper assembly must be removed and replaced on the existing sub-plate using the documented maintenance process in the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual.
not_for: Do not use this runbook for gripper replacement only.
Do not use this runbook for counter-balance weight removal only.
Do not use this runbook for startup-only actions without tipper replacement.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, remove access panels as needed, disconnect and reconnect services, support and remove the tipper, install the replacement tipper, reinstall removed components, and restart the operator station using referenced procedures.

safety_notes: Apply lockout/tagout to the tipper before service using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.
Required PPE includes safety glasses and gloves.
Support the tipper during removal from the sub-plate.
Cover the air line and quick disconnect with tape to prevent debris from entering either side of the air line.
access_or_tools_needed: 3/8-in. wrench or socket if side-panel removal is necessary
Access to the station side panel, control box, ClearLink controller, air-line quick disconnect, conduit, and sub-plate mounting screws
Referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure
Referenced Gripper Replacement procedure if gripper removal is performed
Referenced Counter-balance weights procedure if weight removal is performed

access_required: Access to the tipper station side panel
Access to the air-line quick disconnect at the bottom of the column
Access to the conduit and sub-plate mounting screws
system_preconditions: Tipper lockout/tagout can be applied using the referenced procedure
Replacement tipper is available before removal begins

visual_references.description: ClearLink controller and controller box location reference for disconnecting Ethernet and accessing the control box area.
Side-panel and ClearLink controller area reference used as image support for access context noted in the candidate.
Counter-balance weight assembly reference for the optional removal procedure mentioned by the source.

steps.title: Apply lockout/tagout
steps.purpose: Place the tipper in a safe service state before any disconnection or removal work begins.
steps.instruction: Apply lockout/tagout to the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure before performing any replacement work.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and tagged out for maintenance.
steps.healthy_condition: Lockout/tagout is applied and the tipper is in the required service-safe state.
steps.failure_condition: Lockout/tagout cannot be applied using the referenced procedure.

actions.label: Apply LOTO
actions.exact_action: Apply lockout/tagout to the tipper as referenced by the manual's Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if lockout/tagout cannot be applied using the referenced procedure.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Tipper replacement requires LOTO, PPE, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing four hex head screws, installing the new tipper, reconnecting services, and restarting the operator station.
Required PPE includes safety glasses and gloves; LOTO the tipper; support the tipper during removal; tape over the air line and quick disconnect to prevent debris entry.

steps.title: Put on required PPE
steps.purpose: Ensure the technician is using the required personal protective equipment for the replacement task.
steps.instruction: Put on safety glasses and gloves before continuing with the tipper replacement procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: Safety glasses and gloves are worn before service work proceeds.
steps.failure_condition: Required PPE is not available or not in use.

actions.label: Wear PPE
actions.exact_action: Put on the required PPE: safety glasses and gloves.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if required PPE is not available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Tipper replacement requires LOTO, PPE, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing four hex head screws, installing the new tipper, reconnecting services, and restarting the operator station.
Required PPE includes safety glasses and gloves; LOTO the tipper; support the tipper during removal; tape over the air line and quick disconnect to prevent debris entry.

steps.title: Remove station side panel if access is needed
steps.purpose: Gain access to the motor and internal service areas when the panel blocks replacement work.
steps.instruction: If necessary for access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel.
steps.expected_result: The side panel is removed when needed and access to the motor/service area is available.
steps.healthy_condition: The panel is removed cleanly and the required access area is exposed.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel or fasteners cannot be removed as documented.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: If access is needed, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use as image support for the side-panel and ClearLink controller access area referenced in the candidate note.
Controller box location behind the access area after side-panel removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the side panel cannot be removed as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.

steps.title: Disconnect power cable at the control box
steps.purpose: Separate electrical service from the tipper before removal.
steps.instruction: Remove the control box and disconnect the power cable at the 6-pin Molex connector, then push the cable into the column.
steps.expected_result: The power cable is disconnected and moved into the column.
steps.healthy_condition: The 6-pin Molex power connection is disconnected and the cable is clear of the tipper.
steps.failure_condition: The control box or power cable cannot be disconnected as documented.

actions.label: Remove control box
actions.exact_action: Remove the control box.

actions.label: Disconnect power cable
actions.exact_action: Disconnect the power cable at the 6-pin Molex connector, then push the cable into the column.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Control box and ClearLink controller location used for service disconnection context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the control box or power connection cannot be disconnected as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Tipper replacement requires LOTO, PPE, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing four hex head screws, installing the new tipper, reconnecting services, and restarting the operator station.

steps.title: Disconnect Ethernet from the ClearLink controller
steps.purpose: Separate network service from the tipper before removal.
steps.instruction: Unplug the Ethernet cable from the ClearLink controller and push it into the column.
steps.expected_result: The Ethernet cable is disconnected and moved into the column.
steps.healthy_condition: The Ethernet cable is unplugged from the ClearLink controller and clear of the tipper.
steps.failure_condition: The Ethernet cable cannot be unplugged or routed clear as documented.

actions.label: Disconnect Ethernet
actions.exact_action: Unplug the Ethernet cable from the ClearLink controller and push it into the column.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: ClearLink controller location for Ethernet disconnection.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the Ethernet connection cannot be disconnected as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Tipper replacement requires LOTO, PPE, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing four hex head screws, installing the new tipper, reconnecting services, and restarting the operator station.

steps.title: Disconnect the air line at the quick disconnect
steps.purpose: Separate pneumatic service from the tipper before removal.
steps.instruction: Disconnect the air line at the quick disconnect at the bottom of the column, leaving the quick disconnect with the tipper side of the air line.
steps.expected_result: The air line is disconnected at the bottom of the column.
steps.healthy_condition: The air line is separated and the quick disconnect remains with the tipper side of the air line.
steps.failure_condition: The air line or quick disconnect cannot be disconnected as documented.

actions.label: Disconnect air line
actions.exact_action: Disconnect the air line at the quick disconnect at the bottom of the column, leaving the quick disconnect with the tipper side of the air line.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Pneumatic service area context for locating the air-line service path.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the air line or quick disconnect cannot be disconnected as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Tipper replacement requires LOTO, PPE, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing four hex head screws, installing the new tipper, reconnecting services, and restarting the operator station.
Required PPE includes safety glasses and gloves; LOTO the tipper; support the tipper during removal; tape over the air line and quick disconnect to prevent debris entry.

steps.title: Tape over the air line and quick disconnect
steps.purpose: Prevent debris contamination from entering the disconnected pneumatic line and fitting.
steps.instruction: Add tape over the air line and quick disconnect to prevent debris from entering either side of the air line.
steps.expected_result: The disconnected air line and quick disconnect are protected from contamination.
steps.healthy_condition: Tape fully covers the exposed air-line and quick-disconnect openings.
steps.failure_condition: The disconnected air line remains exposed to debris contamination.

actions.label: Protect air line
actions.exact_action: Add tape over the air line and quick disconnect to prevent debris from entering either side of the air line.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if contamination protection cannot be applied to the disconnected air line.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Required PPE includes safety glasses and gloves; LOTO the tipper; support the tipper during removal; tape over the air line and quick disconnect to prevent debris entry.

steps.title: Disconnect conduit and free service cables
steps.purpose: Fully separate the tipper from the incoming power, air, and Ethernet service bundle.
steps.instruction: Fully disconnect the conduit bringing power, air, and Ethernet to the column, and pull these cables free from the tipper.
steps.expected_result: The conduit and service bundle are disconnected and free from the tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: Power, air, and Ethernet services are no longer attached to the tipper.
steps.failure_condition: The conduit or service bundle cannot be disconnected or freed as documented.

actions.label: Disconnect conduit
actions.exact_action: Fully disconnect the conduit bringing power, air, and Ethernet to the column.

actions.label: Free service cables
actions.exact_action: Pull these cables free from the tipper.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if service connections or conduit cannot be disconnected as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Tipper replacement requires LOTO, PPE, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing four hex head screws, installing the new tipper, reconnecting services, and restarting the operator station.

steps.title: Optionally remove gripper and counter-balance weights
steps.purpose: Reduce handling difficulty or improve access when replacing the tipper.
steps.instruction: If needed for safer or easier replacement, remove the gripper and the counter-balance weights using the referenced procedures.
steps.expected_result: Optional related components are removed only when needed for access or safer handling.
steps.healthy_condition: Any optional removals are completed using the referenced procedures.
steps.failure_condition: The optional related components cannot be removed using the referenced procedures.

actions.label: Remove gripper if needed
actions.exact_action: Optionally remove the gripper using the referenced procedure if needed for safer or easier replacement.

actions.label: Remove counter-balance weights if needed
actions.exact_action: Optionally remove the counter-balance weights using the referenced procedure if needed for safer or easier replacement.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counter-balance weight assembly referenced for the optional removal procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if optional related components cannot be removed using the referenced procedures.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Optional but recommended: remove the gripper and the counter-balance weights; mount the gripper again during installation if necessary.
Figure 7-4 appears on page 111 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_3_1_removal.

steps.title: Support the tipper and remove mounting screws
steps.purpose: Safely detach the existing tipper from the sub-plate without dropping or shifting the assembly.
steps.instruction: Support the tipper and remove the four hex head screws holding the tipper to the sub-plate.
steps.expected_result: The existing tipper is detached from the sub-plate while supported.
steps.healthy_condition: The tipper remains supported throughout removal and the four mounting screws are removed.
steps.failure_condition: The tipper cannot be safely supported during removal or the mounting screws cannot be removed.

actions.label: Support tipper
actions.exact_action: Support the tipper.

actions.label: Remove mounting screws
actions.exact_action: Remove the four hex head screws holding the tipper to the sub-plate.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the tipper cannot be safely supported during removal.
Escalate if mounting hardware cannot be disconnected as documented.

steps.healthy_condition: Conduit, power, air, and Ethernet are restored to the column and control box.
steps.failure_condition: Any service connection or conduit cannot be reconnected as documented.

steps.title: Reinstall the gripper if it was removed
steps.purpose: Restore any optional component removed earlier for access or safer handling.
steps.instruction: Mount the gripper again if it was removed.
steps.expected_result: The gripper is reinstalled when applicable.
steps.healthy_condition: The gripper is mounted again if it was removed earlier.
steps.failure_condition: The gripper cannot be remounted using the referenced procedure.

actions.exact_action: Mount the gripper again if it was removed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the gripper cannot be remounted as required.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Optional but recommended: remove the gripper and the counter-balance weights; mount the gripper again during installation if necessary.

steps.instruction: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels if they were removed.
steps.healthy_condition: Any removed top and/or bottom side panels are back in place.
steps.failure_condition: Removed side panels cannot be reinstalled.

actions.exact_action: Reinstall the top and/or bottom side panels if they were removed.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if removed panels cannot be reinstalled.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Optional but recommended removal of the gripper and counter-balance weights is referenced, and side panels may be removed for access and reinstalled afterward.

steps.title: Restart the operator station
steps.purpose: Return the station to operation after the replacement and reconnection work is complete.
steps.instruction: Restart the operator station using the referenced startup procedure.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is restarted after installation.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station restarts using the referenced procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The operator station cannot be restarted after installation.

actions.label: Restart station
actions.exact_action: Restart the operator station using the referenced startup procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the operator station cannot be restarted after installation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Tipper replacement requires LOTO, PPE, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing four hex head screws, installing the new tipper, reconnecting services, and restarting the operator station.

success_criteria: The replacement tipper is mounted to the existing sub-plate.
Required power, Ethernet, air, and conduit connections are restored.
Removed panels or related components are reinstalled as needed.
healthy_conditions: Lockout/tagout is applied before service begins.
The tipper is supported during removal.
The air line and quick disconnect are protected from debris after disconnection.
The replacement tipper is installed on the existing sub-plate.
Power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services are reconnected.
The operator station restarts using the referenced procedure.
failure_conditions: Lockout/tagout cannot be applied using the referenced procedure.
The tipper cannot be safely supported during removal.
Service connections, conduit, or mounting hardware cannot be disconnected or reconnected as documented.
The operator station cannot be restarted after installation.
escalation_guidance: Stop the procedure if lockout/tagout cannot be applied using the referenced procedure.
Stop the procedure if the tipper cannot be safely supported during removal.
Escalate if service connections, conduit, or mounting hardware cannot be disconnected or reconnected as documented.
Escalate if the operator station cannot be restarted after installation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: ClearLink controller and control box location for service disconnection and reconnection context.
Side-panel and controller-area access context used to support the candidate's image note.
Counter-balance weight assembly for the optional related removal procedure.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Tipper replacement requires LOTO, PPE, disconnecting power, Ethernet, air, and conduit services, supporting the tipper while removing four hex head screws, installing the new tipper, reconnecting services, and restarting the operator station.
If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel to access the motor.
Optional but recommended: remove the gripper and the counter-balance weights; mount the gripper again during installation if necessary.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide the full verbatim page text for section 7.3.22.
The source does not provide torque values for reinstallation fasteners.
The source does not provide a documented estimated completion time for tipper replacement.
The source does not provide explicit post-restart verification checks or status indicators.
The source does not provide detailed instructions for control box removal beyond the candidate summary and related artifact context.
The source does not provide explicit criteria for when gripper or counter-balance weight removal is required versus optional.
